prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
Item "9A. Controls and Procedures" ENDS on this page; the next section is "Item 9B. Other Information".
First locate where Item 9A stops on this page:
- If the "Item 9B" heading is visible on this page, Item 9A ends on the line directly ABOVE that heading.
- Ignore ALL text below that heading; it belongs to the next section, not Item 9A.
- The target section may have started on a previous page and may leave only a short carryover at the very TOP of this page.
- If the next-section heading appears near the top, the answer may be only the few lines immediately above that heading.
- Only if no later section heading appears on this page should you use the bottom of the page as Item 9A's ending.
Then transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 9A right before the section ends. The answer must come only from Item 9A, even if the next section occupies most of the page. Ignore running headers, page numbers, footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
